title: Short-Term Rentals: Airbnb's Dominance and Booking's Gains
text: Airbnb achieved a 44% market share in 2024, far ahead of competitors. Airbnb's market share rose from 28% in 2019 to 44% in 2024, while Booking.com increased from 14% to 18% and Vrbo decreased from 11% to 9%. Airbnb leads the industry with a 44% market share, significantly ahead of Booking.com (18%) and Expedia/Vrbo (9%). As a digital-first platform, Airbnb delivers transparent pricing, real-time reviews, secure identity verification, and frictionless booking. By 2025, its advanced AI-driven recommendation system analyses user behavior and preferences to suggest tailored listings, increasing satisfaction by 30% and boosting booking completion by 25%. Airbnb's asset-light business model empowers homeowners to rent out spare rooms or entire properties, offering travelers unique accommodations often at lower prices than hotels. The platform charges hosts a 3-5% commission and applies variable guest service fees. Airbnb facilitates 500 million annual bookings with total debt at $2.28 billion, cash reserves reaching $11.5 billion and free cash flow at $4.4 billion.
